            <name>Tofu Teriyaki Donburi</name>
            <short_description>Pan-seared teriyaki-glazed tofu served over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and pickled ginger — a plant-based, savory rice bowl option.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Press and slice firm tofu into steaks or cubes. Pan-sear in a little oil until golden on both sides. Serve over steamed rice with steamed broccoli and a small mound of pickled ginger. Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced green onion. Overall a balanced, nutrient-dense plant-based bowl—add edamame or a side salad to further increase protein and fiber if desired.</nutrition_note>

            <meal_suggestion>Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Toss potatoes and green beans with olive oil, salt, and pepper, and spread on a sheet pan.               <item name="cheddar" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Balanced: lean turkey provides protein, rice offers carbohydrates, and peppers add fiber; cheddar adds calcium and flavor. Monitor sodium from tomato sauce; lighter options include lean turkey, brown rice, and reduced-fat cheese.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Baked Salmon Plate — Berry Boost Twist</name>
            <short_description>A baked salmon plate with lemon, vegetables, and rice, plus an optional Berry Boost Twist by adding berries for extra antioxidants.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Bake a 4–6 oz salmon fillet with lemon. Serve with 1 cup cooked rice and 1–2 cups mixed vegetables. For the Berry Boost Twist, add a handful of fresh berries on the side or a light berry glaze.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="salmon" />
              <item name="lemon" />
              <item name="mixed vegetables" />
              <item name="rice" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Protein-rich salmon provides omega-3 fats (EPA/DHA) and high-quality protein. Mixed vegetables supply fiber and micronutrients; lemon adds vitamin C. Rice contributes carbohydrates for energy; brown rice can boost fiber. Berry Boost Twist suggests adding berries (blueberries, strawberries, etc.) for antioxidant benefits.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Dinner>
      </Day>
      <Day index="4">
        <Breakfast>
          <Meal>
            <name>Oatmeal — Smoky Variation</name>
            <short_description>Creamy milk-based oatmeal with banana and almonds, offering a balanced, fiber-rich start to the day with a subtle smoky variation.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>A warm, smoky-flavored oatmeal with banana and almonds. Serve hot; for extra depth, add a pinch of cinnamon or a light drizzle of honey or maple syrup.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="rolled oats" />
              <item name="milk" />
              <item name="banana" />
              <item name="almonds" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Balanced breakfast with complex carbs (oats), protein and calcium (milk), potassium and natural sweetness (banana), and healthy fats/protein (almonds). Optional adjustments: use skim milk for lower calories or whole milk for more creaminess.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Overnight Oats — Mild-Spice Set</name>
            <short_description>Creamy overnight oats with apple and cinnamon—a fiber- and protein-rich, mildly spiced breakfast.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>For more protein, mix in Greek yogurt or a protein powder. Boost fiber and omega-3s with chia or flax seeds. Top with extra diced apple or a pinch more cinnamon. To make dairy-free, use plant milk and yogurt alternatives.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="rolled oats" />
              <item name="milk" />
              <item name="plain yogurt" />
              <item name="apple" />
              <item name="cinnamon" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Balanced mix of fiber (oats, apple), protein and calcium (milk and yogurt); cinnamon adds flavor with minimal sugar. To lower sugar, use unsweetened dairy or dairy-free alternatives and skip any additional sweeteners.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Whole-Grain Pancakes — Lemon-Herb Skillet</name>
            <short_description>Whole-grain pancakes with eggs and milk, topped with berries and a light maple sweetness.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Pair with a side of yogurt or extra berries to boost protein and fiber; limit maple syrup to 1–2 tsp or swap for a berry compote; add lemon zest or fresh herbs to echo the lemon-herb concept without adding sugar.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="whole-wheat flour" />
              <item name="egg" />
              <item name="milk" />
              <item name="berries" />
              <item name="maple syrup" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Whole-wheat flour provides fiber; eggs and milk contribute protein and calcium; berries supply vitamins and antioxidants; maple syrup adds sugars—use sparingly or pair with extra fruit to moderate sweetness.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Breakfast>
        <Lunch>
          <Meal>
            <name>Black Bean Wrap — Garden Veg Skillet</name>
            <short_description>A fiber-rich, plant-based wrap featuring black beans, avocado, lettuce, and salsa in a whole-wheat tortilla.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Great quick lunch or light dinner. Enhance protein with a dollop of Greek yogurt or sprinkle of nutritional yeast, and add more veggies or a side salad for balance.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="whole-wheat wrap" />
              <item name="black beans" />
              <item name="avocado" />
              <item name="lettuce" />
              <item name="salsa" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Plant-based, high in fiber and plant protein from black beans; healthy fats from avocado; whole-wheat wrap adds more fiber. Salsa and lettuce contribute volume with relatively low calories. Watch sodium if using store-bought salsa.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Chicken Rice Bowl — Maple-Kissed Variation</name>
            <short_description>A simple chicken rice bowl with broccoli and soy seasoning—protein, carbs, and vegetables in one dish, with mindful sodium and potential maple variation.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Grill or sauté chicken, steam broccoli, and serve over cooked rice. Lightly drizzle with soy sauce. For a lower-sodium option, use low-sodium soy sauce and consider brown rice for more fiber.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="chicken" />
              <item name="rice" />
              <item name="broccoli" />
              <item name="soy sauce" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Protein from chicken, carbs from rice, and fiber from broccoli create a balanced plate. Soy sauce adds sodium; the Maple-Kissed name hints at sweetness not present in the listed ingredients, so adding maple would change the nutrition profile.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Soba Noodle Bowl — Berry Boost Variation</name>
            <short_description>Chilled soba noodles with cucumber, carrot, and egg, tossed in sesame dressing.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve cold or at room temperature; consider adding berries to reflect the Berry Boost variation and/or increase protein with extra egg or tofu; adjust dressing for desired fat content.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="soba noodles" />
              <item name="cucumber" />
              <item name="carrots" />
              <item name="egg" />
              <item name="sesame dressing" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Balanced meal with complex carbs from soba, protein from egg, and fiber/micronutrients from cucumber and carrot. Sesame dressing provides healthy fats; the Berry Boost label isn’t represented by these ingredients unless berries are added.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Lunch>
        <Dinner>
          <Meal>
            <name>Vegetable Soup & Bread — Garden Veg Variation</name>
            <short_description>A plant-forward combination of vegetable soup, whole grain bread, and a side salad for fiber, nutrients, and steady energy.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve 1 cup of vegetable soup with 1 to 2 slices of whole grain bread and a side salad. For extra protein, add beans, lentils, or tofu to the soup or include a protein-rich topping on the salad.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="vegetable soup" />
              <item name="whole-grain bread" />
              <item name="side salad" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Plant-forward, fiber-rich meal with vegetables, whole grains, and greens. High in dietary fiber and vitamins; provides moderate protein from soup and bread. To keep sodium in check, choose a low-sodium vegetable soup. Optional protein boost (beans, lentils, or tofu) can be added for more protein.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Stuffed Bell Peppers — Citrus Fresh Combo</name>
            <short_description>Bell peppers stuffed with lean turkey, rice, tomato sauce, and cheddar—a balanced mix of protein, starch, and vegetables.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Serve one stuffed pepper per person with a simple side salad. For a lighter version, use brown rice and reduced-fat cheddar; choose low-sodium tomato sauce. Bake until peppers are tender.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="bell peppers" />
              <item name="ground turkey" />
              <item name="rice" />
              <item name="tomato sauce" />
              <item name="cheddar" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>High in protein from lean ground turkey; includes complex carbs from rice and fiber from peppers. Cheddar adds fat and calcium. Tomato sauce provides lycopene; to manage sodium, use low-sodium sauce and consider brown rice for extra fiber.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>DIY Taco Night — Garden Veg Wrap</name>
            <short_description>DIY Taco Night Garden Veg Wrap: lean ground beef, corn tortillas, lettuce, tomato, cheddar, and salsa wrapped into a protein-rich, veggie-forward option.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>To boost nutrition: use whole-grain tortillas, add a bean or avocado side, and load with extra veggies. Choose low-sodium salsa and cheese or reduce cheese. Serve with a simple side salad or fruit.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="lean ground beef" />
              <item name="corn tortillas" />
              <item name="lettuce" />
              <item name="tomato" />
              <item name="cheddar" />
              <item name="salsa" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Protein-rich meal from lean ground beef and cheddar; includes veggies (lettuce, tomato) and carbs from corn tortillas. Moderate sodium from cheese and salsa. For more fiber and heart-healthy fats, swap to whole-grain tortillas and add beans or avocado, and opt for lower-sodium cheese/salsa.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Zucchini Noodle Pesto Bowl</name>
            <short_description>Light, vegetable-forward dinner of zucchini noodles tossed with basil-parmesan pesto, cherry tomatoes, toasted pine nuts, and a sprinkle of parmesan — quick, low-carb, and flavorful.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Use a spiralizer or vegetable peeler to make zucchini noodles (zoodles). In a food processor, blend fresh basil, garlic, toasted pine nuts, grated Parmesan, lemon juice, and extra-virgin olive oil until smooth to make a bright pesto. Toss zoodles briefly in a warm skillet with a little olive oil (1–2 minutes) to soften slightly, then remove from heat and toss with the pesto, halved cherry tomatoes, and a small handful of toasted pine nuts. Finish with a light grate of Parmesan and a squeeze of lemon. Optional: add grilled shrimp or chickpeas for extra protein.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="zucchini" />
              <item name="basil" />
              <item name="garlic" />
              <item name="pine nuts" />
              <item name="Parmesan" />
              <item name="extra-virgin olive oil" />
              <item name="lemon juice" />
              <item name="cherry tomatoes" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Low in carbohydrates and calories while high in vegetable volume and micronutrients. Zucchini supplies fiber and vitamin C; basil offers antioxidants; olive oil and pine nuts provide heart-healthy monounsaturated fats; Parmesan adds calcium and savory umami. Adding grilled shrimp or chickpeas increases protein for a more complete meal. Overall, a light, Mediterranean-style dinner aligned with vegetable-forward and balanced eating.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
        </Dinner>
      </Day>
    </Person>"
}
